metadata.title: Accretion Disk Physics Models
metadata.description: Comprehensive database of accretion disk models, temperature profiles, luminosity calculations, and radiation mechanisms for black hole accretion

accretionPower.description: Accretion luminosity is the mass accretion rate times c² times the radiative efficiency η

bondiAccretion.description: Spherical accretion rate from ambient medium with density ρ_∞ and sound speed c_s

alphaParameter.description: Shakura-Sunyaev α parameter: parameterizes turbulent viscosity as ν = α c_s H

typicalValues.note: α is now understood to arise from the MRI (Magnetorotational Instability)

MRI.name: Magnetorotational Instability
MRI.discoveredBy: Velikhov (1959), Chandrasekhar (1960), rediscovered by Balbus & Hawley (1991)
MRI.description: Weak magnetic fields in a differentially rotating disk are amplified by the MRI, creating turbulence that transports angular momentum outward
MRI.wavelengthMostUnstable: λ = 2π v_A / Ω where v_A is Alfvén speed

effectiveAlpha.note: MRI-driven turbulence produces effective α ~ 0.01-0.1 in simulations

shakuraSunyaevThinDisk.name: Shakura-Sunyaev α-disk (Standard Thin Disk)
assumptions: Radiatively efficient: all viscous heating radiated locally
Steady-state: Ṁ = constant through the disk

luminosityProfile.halfLuminosityRadius: r ≈ 4.5 R_in (half the luminosity comes from inside this radius)

surfaceDensityProfile.note: Surface density has a maximum and then drops near ISCO

spectrum.spectralShape: Modified blackbody: F_ν ∝ ν^(1/3) exp(-hν/kT_max) for hν << kT_max and exponential cutoff at high energies
spectrum.lowEnergy: F_ν ∝ ν^(1/3) (Rayleigh-Jeans regime modified by temperature gradient)

temperatureExamplesForDifferentMasses.note: Thin disk model NOT applicable — Sgr A* and M87* are in RIAF regime

diskColorsForVisualization.description: Approximate RGB colors of different disk annuli for a 10^8 M_sun BH accreting at 10% Eddington

validity.upperLimit: L/L_Edd ~ 0.3 — above this, radiation pressure important (slim disk regime)

novikovThorneModel.name: Novikov-Thorne Relativistic Thin Disk
novikovThorneModel.reference: Novikov & Thorne, in 'Black Holes' (1973); Page & Thorne, ApJ 191, 499 (1974)
novikovThorneModel.description: Full general relativistic version of the thin disk model including GR corrections to the energy, angular momentum, and torque
improvements_over_SS73: Proper GR metric used for orbital dynamics
Light bending (ray tracing needed for observed flux)
ISCO stress boundary condition in curved spacetime
Frame dragging for Kerr black holes

fluxFormula.description: The local flux from the disk surface
fluxFormula.formula: F(r) = -Ṁ/(4π√(-g)) × Ω,r / (E-ΩL)² × ∫[R_in to r] (E-ΩL) L,r dr
fluxFormula.note: E, L, Ω are the specific energy, angular momentum, and angular velocity of circular orbits

GRcorrectionFactors.description: Correction factors relative to Newtonian disk
GRcorrectionFactors.note: GR effects make the inner disk brighter and hotter than the Newtonian prediction

ADAF.name: Advection-Dominated Accretion Flow
ADAF.description: At low accretion rates, the disk becomes optically thin and geometrically thick. Most of the viscous heating energy is advected with the flow into the black hole rather than radiated

Te_over_Ti.note: Electrons are much cooler than ions

luminosity.scaling: L ∝ Ṁ² (quadratic in accretion rate, much less efficient than thin disk L ∝ Ṁ)
luminosity.explanation: Because radiative efficiency drops with decreasing Ṁ, the luminosity falls faster than linearly

temperatureProfile.electrons: T_e ≈ const ≈ few × 10⁹ K (electron temperature saturates via Coulomb coupling floor)
temperatureProfile.ions: T_i ∝ r⁻¹ (virial temperature, ions are nearly virial)

CDAF.name: Convection-Dominated Accretion Flow
CDAF.description: At very low Ṁ, convection transports energy outward faster than advection carries it inward

ADIOS.name: Advection-Dominated Inflow-Outflow Solution
ADIOS.description: Significant mass loss in winds/outflows, so Ṁ decreases inward as Ṁ ∝ r^p

MAD.name: Magnetically Arrested Disk
MAD.reference: Narayan, Igumenshchev & Abramowicz, PASJ 55, L69 (2003); Tchekhovskoy, Narayan & McKinney, MNRAS 418, L79 (2011)
MAD.description: When the magnetic flux on the black hole reaches saturation, the magnetic pressure at the horizon becomes strong enough to obstruct the accretion flow

jetEfficiency.description: MAD state can extract rotational energy of the black hole via the Blandford-Znajek process
jetEfficiency.maxJetEfficiency: η_jet > 100% of Ṁc² for rapid spin (extracting BH spin energy)
jetEfficiency.scalingWithSpin: P_jet ∝ a² Φ_BH² (jet power scales with spin² and magnetic flux²)
jetEfficiency.note: Efficiencies >100% possible because energy is extracted from BH spin, not just accretion

observationalEvidence.M87: EHT polarimetry strongly suggests MAD state
observationalEvidence.jetPower_vs_accretionPower: Many AGN jets show P_jet >> L_disk, requiring MAD-like extraction

SANE.name: Standard And Normal Evolution
SANE.description: The 'normal' accretion state where magnetic flux on the BH is below the MAD saturation limit

slimDisk.name: Slim Disk (Super-Eddington Accretion)
slimDisk.description: At super-Eddington accretion rates, radiation is trapped in the flow and advected inward

properties.luminosity: L ~ L_Edd × [1 + ln(Ṁ/Ṁ_Edd)] (logarithmic increase above Eddington)
properties.temperatureProfile: T ∝ r⁻¹/² (flatter than thin disk r⁻³/⁴)

applications: Early rapid growth of supermassive BH seeds

blandfordZnajek.name: Blandford-Znajek Process
blandfordZnajek.description: Electromagnetic extraction of black hole rotational energy via large-scale magnetic fields threading the event horizon

where.Omega_H: Angular velocity of the horizon = ac/(2Mr_+)

blandfordZnajek.spinDependence: P_BZ ∝ a² for small spin, saturates for high spin
blandfordZnajek.note: This is the leading model for relativistic jets in AGN and GRBs

blandfordPayne.name: Blandford-Payne Process
blandfordPayne.description: Centrifugally driven wind/jet from the disk surface along open magnetic field lines
blandfordPayne.criticalAngle_note: Field lines must make less than 60° with the disk plane to launch a wind
blandfordPayne.produces: Mildly relativistic disk winds rather than highly relativistic jets

jetComposition.M87jet: Likely pair-dominated near base, with entrainment of baryonic matter further out
jetComposition.evidence: Jet polarization, Faraday rotation, emission spectra constrain composition

GRMHDsimulations.description: General Relativistic Magnetohydrodynamic simulations are the gold standard for modeling black hole accretion

rayTracingCodes.name: BHOSS
rayTracingCodes.description: Black Hole Observations with Simulated Spectra

rayTracingCodes.name: ipole
rayTracingCodes.description: Polarized ray tracing with Stokes parameters

rayTracingCodes.name: RAPTOR
rayTracingCodes.description: Relativistic Astrophysics of Pulsars and Tori

rayTracingCodes.name: Gyoto
rayTracingCodes.description: General relativitY Orbit Tracer of Observatoire de Paris

typicalResults.turbulentDisk: MRI-driven turbulence develops after ~10 orbital periods
typicalResults.jetFormation: Spontaneous jet formation in MAD state with spinning BH
typicalResults.resolution: Typical grid: 256×128×128 (r,θ,φ) in 3D, with AMR up to effective 4096³

synchrotron.description: Radiation from relativistic electrons spiraling in magnetic fields
synchrotron.spectralIndex: F_ν ∝ ν^(-(p-1)/2) for power-law electron distribution ∝ γ^(-p)
synchrotron.selfAbsorption: At low frequencies, synchrotron becomes self-absorbed: F_ν ∝ ν^(5/2)

inverseCompton.description: Low-energy photons upscattered by hot electrons
inverseCompton.spectrumModification: Creates power-law X-ray tail above the disk thermal peak

comptonization.description: Repeated Compton scatterings create a power-law spectrum

thermalInstability.description: Radiation-pressure-dominated inner disk regions are thermally unstable in the α-prescription
thermalInstability.condition: Radiation pressure > gas pressure, which occurs for L/L_Edd > 0.01-0.1

viscousInstability.description: Disk may be secularly unstable if dΣ/dṀ < 0
viscousInstability.relatedTo: Dwarf nova outbursts, X-ray transient state transitions

diskTearing.description: Misaligned disks around spinning BHs can be torn apart by Lense-Thirring precession
diskTearing.reference: Nixon et al. (2012), Liska et al. (2021)

sources: Novikov & Thorne, in 'Black Holes' ed. DeWitt & DeWitt (1973)
Abramowicz & Fragile, Living Reviews in Relativity 16, 1 (2013)
